methodology: Strictly chronological: searched day-by-day news before forming judgment, then verified actual exit prices using yfinance historical data 20 calendar days post-entry. No forward-looking data used at decision time.

macro_context.fomc_october_29: Fed cut 25bp to 3.75-4.00% on Oct 29 but Powell hawkish press conference — said Dec cut not certain. 10 of 12 members voted for cut, Miran wanted 50bp, Schmid wanted hold.
macro_context.government_shutdown: US government shutdown Oct 1 - Nov 13 (43 days, longest in history). Disrupted BLS CPI data collection for Oct and half of Nov.
macro_context.ai_earnings_season: Q3 FY2026 earnings season largely complete by early Nov. AMD reported Nov 4 (beat). CEG/VST/NRG reported Nov 6-7. NVDA Q3 due Nov 19.
macro_context.key_macro_event_nov_13: Multiple regional Fed presidents (Boston, Cleveland, St. Louis) hawkish commentary on Nov 13. Dec cut odds collapsed from near-certainty (~90%) to ~50%. Tech/AI selloff third 1%+ decline in two weeks.
macro_context.market_regime: AI multiple compression regime. November 2025: S&P 500 declined 5.7% from Oct high before recovering to +0.2% on month. Tech -4.3%, Healthcare +9.3% (best month in 3 years). Sector rotation defining theme.

portfolio_note: Equal-weighted notional. Shorts counted as gains when price falls (pnl_pct = (entry-exit)/entry*100 for shorts). Puts modeled as underlying directional P&L proxy.

best_trade.summary: AMD short from 253.47 entered Nov 6 on sell-the-news post Q3 earnings beat. AMD fell to 214.24 by Nov 26 as hawkish Fed repricing + AI multiple compression compounded. Classic earnings-already-priced-in setup.

worst_trade.summary: NVDA long from 206.86 on Nov 3 AI deal news. NVDA reported blowout Q3 Nov 19 ($57B revenue, +62% YoY) but stock sold off violently (sell-the-news). Combined with hawkish Fed multiple compression, fell to 182.53 by Nov 24.

trades.thesis: OpenAI signed $38B AWS deal tapping hundreds of thousands of Nvidia GPUs. Micron up ~5%, NVDA up 2% on the day. Q3 FY2026 earnings due Nov 19 as known catalyst. AI demand at peak momentum — pre-earnings run typical for NVDA.
trades.catalyst: OpenAI-AWS $38B GPU capacity deal announced Nov 3; Iren-MSFT $9.7B GB300 GPU deal; NVDA Q3 FY2026 earnings scheduled Nov 19
trades.lesson: Pre-earnings AI run reversed post-earnings. NVDA reported blowout Q3 on Nov 19 ($57B rev, +62% YoY, Data Center $51.2B +66% YoY) but stock sold off violently — classic sell-the-news. Hawkish Fed commentary Nov 13 (Dec cut odds dropped from ~90% to ~50%) accelerated tech derating. Should have taken profit on Nov 10 bounce to ~199.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: Market priced in perfection pre-earnings; hawkish Fed repricing collapsed Dec cut odds mid-month; NVDA post-earnings sell-the-news with stock -12% from pre-earnings levels by Nov 21; tech multiple compression the dominant November theme

trades.thesis: AMD Q3 earnings reported Nov 4 after close: beat revenue ($9.2B, +36% YoY) and EPS ($1.20 adj). Q4 guidance above consensus ($9.6B vs $9.17B est). But classic sell-the-news pattern — stock already up 50%+ YTD. AMD recovered Nov 5 (243 open, 256 close), setting up Nov 6 fade. AI multiple compression beginning across sector.
trades.catalyst: Earnings beat already priced in; stretched valuations post-50% YTD gain; start of tech/AI derating; Oct 29 FOMC hawkish press conference dampening Dec rate-cut expectations
trades.lesson: Sell-the-news after big earnings run is reliable when: (1) stock up 50%+ YTD, (2) beat was consensus-expected, (3) macro backdrop tightening via hawkish Fed. AMD fell from 253 to 203 (-20%) by Nov 21 as hawkish Fed + tech multiple compression compounded. Exited at 214.24 on Nov 26 for +15.5%.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: Market repricing AI multiples as Dec rate cut odds collapsed from near-certainty to ~50%; AMD hit by fear of losing GPU share to NVDA in next cycle; profit-taking by momentum funds post-earnings season; short interest building as valuation discipline returned

trades.thesis: CEG Q3 earnings due Nov 7. Nuclear power demand surging from data center AI electricity needs. CEG is largest US nuclear fleet operator with nearly 90% carbon-free output. Crane Clean Energy Center restart on track. 20-year PPA deals being signed industry-wide. Stock had pulled back from highs — entry ahead of strong quarterly numbers expected.
trades.catalyst: Q3 earnings Nov 7; nuclear PPA signing momentum; data center electricity structural demand story; NRC license extensions across nuclear fleet
trades.lesson: CEG beat EPS ($3.04 adj, +11% YoY, nuclear capacity factor 96.8%) but narrowed guidance with midpoint BELOW analyst consensus ($9.25 midpoint vs $9.42 consensus). Market focused on guidance miss not operational beat. Stock bounced to 357 on earnings day then broad energy derating pulled it down. Nuclear thesis intact long-term but guidance miss + rising rate environment compressed multiple short-term.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: Guidance midpoint miss ($9.25 vs $9.42 est) trumped operational beat; hawkish Fed removed rate-cut tailwind for utility-like nuclear names; broad equity derating Nov 13 from hawkish Fed officials hit CEG as rate-sensitive name; sector rotation INTO healthcare/staples, OUT of energy

trades.thesis: VST reported Q3 on Nov 6 with strong results: Adj EBITDA +$143M YoY driven by higher energy/capacity prices and nuclear production tax credits. Signed 20-year 1,200MW PPA for Comanche Peak nuclear plant with investment-grade counterparty. NRC approved Perry Nuclear 20-year license extension to 2046. Stock pulled back 5% into earnings (182 to 178). Post-earnings bounce should materialize given structural nuclear demand.
trades.catalyst: Q3 earnings beat; Comanche Peak 20-year PPA with IG counterparty; Perry Nuclear NRC license extension through 2046; data center electricity demand; revenue ~$4.97B
trades.lesson: VST had all right fundamental catalysts but macro environment overwhelmed them. Stock hit 190 on Nov 7 (entry day) then progressively sold to 168 by Nov 21. Should have taken profits at the Nov 7 close spike to 190 (+6.7% in one day). Rate-sensitive power generators sold alongside tech as Dec cut odds dropped from ~90% to ~50%.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: November 2025 sector rotation month: healthcare +9.3% (best in 3 years) vs energy/power lagged. Rate sensitivity dominated VST as utility-adjacent name. Macro headwind from hawkish Fed overwhelmed even strong fundamental catalysts. VST would eventually recover strongly but timing was wrong.

trades.thesis: Multiple signals converging for Nasdaq correction: (1) Oct 29 FOMC hawkish press conference — Powell signaled Dec cut not guaranteed; (2) AMD earnings sell-the-news pattern established; (3) AI/tech valuations stretched at 22.8x forward vs 10yr avg 18.8x; (4) Bitcoin -20% from Oct highs signaling risk appetite peak; (5) November historically sees profit-taking post Q3 earnings season.
trades.catalyst: Hawkish Fed repricing underway; AI multiple compression; earnings season complete removing catalyst; CPI print Nov 13 as binary event; multiple hawkish regional Fed presidents expected to speak
trades.lesson: Directionally right (QQQ fell to 595-607 range Nov 14) but QQQ recovered by Nov 28 to 617. Put theta decay eroded directional gain. Should have exited put on Nov 14 when QQQ was at 595-607 — that was peak of the move. Timing of options exit is as critical as direction. Index recovery was faster than anticipated.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: QQQ dropped hard Nov 13-14 on hawkish Fed commentary and shutdown-disrupted CPI print, then recovered as market priced in Dec cut still possible (~50%). Index volatility high but mean-reverting — puts needed sharper entry or earlier exit to capture the Nov 13-14 leg down fully.

trades.thesis: NRG Q3 earnings due Nov 6 with expected strong results. Company announcing $3B share buyback through 2028 and closed $4.9B financing for LS Power acquisition ($12B EV, doubles generation capacity with 13GW natural gas). Power sector benefiting from data center electricity demand. Asymmetric setup: buyback + earnings + strategic catalyst.
trades.catalyst: Q3 earnings Nov 6; $3B share buyback authorization through 2028; LS Power acquisition financing closed ($4.9B); natural gas generation capacity doubling to 13GW; AI data center electricity demand
trades.lesson: NRG reported solid Q3 with buyback — stock hit 176 on Nov 6 briefly. But same macro headwinds hit energy sector: rising rates, hawkish Fed, sector rotation to healthcare/staples. Power sector stocks traded more on rate sensitivity than fundamental catalysts during November 2025.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: November 2025 sector rotation: healthcare +9.3% vs energy lagged. Rate sensitivity dominated power stocks as Dec cut odds collapsed. NRG fundamental story (buyback, LS Power acquisition) was sound but macro headwind from hawkish Fed repricing was too strong during the 20-day holding period.

trades.thesis: AMD bounced sharply on Nov 12 (open 253, closed 258) on reports of Microsoft AI chip orders and technical support at 250. After -10% from earnings peak, stock technically oversold. Short-term mean reversion play — not a conviction long. Tactical trade to capture the Nov 12 technical bounce.
trades.catalyst: Technical oversold bounce at 250 support level; Microsoft AI chip order reports; Nov 12 S&P sector partial recovery; high volume on the day indicating institutional buying interest
trades.lesson: Oversold bounce thesis failed as hawkish Fed commentary on Nov 13 triggered broad selloff. AMD fell from 258 close Nov 12 to 247 by Nov 14. Mean reversion in a deteriorating macro environment is dangerous — the downtrend was intact, bounce not supported by fundamental reversal. Should have maintained short-only posture in AMD throughout the period.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: Nov 13 hawkish Fed officials (Boston, Cleveland, St. Louis presidents all hawkish) hit tech hardest. Market in distribution phase, not dip-buying regime. S&P fell 1%+ for third time in two weeks — technical damage accumulated, not recovered.

trades.thesis: PLTR trading at extreme valuations (~100x forward earnings) after parabolic run from $40 to $207. Government shutdown ending Nov 13 removes near-term government contract catalyst (shutdown-related contract pause created artificial scarcity). No clear binary catalyst to sustain valuation post-shutdown. Classic momentum exhaustion setup.
trades.catalyst: Government shutdown ending Nov 13 (sell-the-news on contract backlog clearing); hawkish Fed removing liquidity narrative for high-multiple names; extreme valuation at 100x+ forward earnings; Bitcoin -20% from Oct peak as leading risk-off indicator; broader tech multiple compression
trades.lesson: Short high-multiple momentum stocks when: (1) macro tailwind reverses (rate cuts re-priced away), (2) known catalyst passes (government shutdown ends = sell-the-news), (3) broad tech derating underway. PLTR fell from 193 to 155 at its worst (-20%). Exited at 168.45 for +13.0% on the short.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: PLTR institutional holders sold on government shutdown resolution; hawkish Fed removed liquidity narrative supporting speculative high-multiple names; bitcoin -20% from Oct peak was the leading indicator of risk-off shift; every Magnificent 7 except Alphabet posted losing week in late Nov

trades.thesis: SPY oversold after 3 consecutive 1%+ down sessions in two weeks. Government shutdown ended Nov 13 (43-day longest in history) — historically S&P rallies post-shutdown resolution. November historically strongest month for S&P (+1.8% avg per Stock Trader's Almanac). Positioning too bearish after hawkish Fed commentary. Mean reversion to 680 level expected.
trades.catalyst: Government shutdown resolution Nov 13; technically oversold at -2.5% from recent high; November seasonality; Dec cut odds still ~50% (not zero) — overshoot to downside on hawkish repricing; earnings uncertainty cleared with Q3 season largely complete
trades.lesson: Post-government-shutdown resolution bounce trade worked cleanly. SPY rallied from 661 to 680 by Dec 3 (+2.8%). Hawkish Fed concerns were already priced in after the sharp Nov 13 selloff. Shutdown-disrupted CPI created unreliable data, causing market to overshoot to downside. Contrarian long on third 1%+ decline in two weeks is high-probability setup.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: Market oversold on hawkish Fed repricing but Dec cut was never fully removed; S&P recovered as investors recognized shutdown-disrupted CPI was unreliable; November seasonality kicked in; healthcare and staples led, broad index recovered even as tech remained under pressure

trades.thesis: MSFT directly named in two AI infrastructure deals on Nov 3: (1) OpenAI-AWS $38B deal (MSFT-backed OpenAI scales AI infrastructure), (2) Iren-MSFT $9.7B deal for Nvidia GB300 GPU access. AI capex cycle accelerating with MSFT as central infrastructure beneficiary. Q3 earnings already reported (beat). November seasonality positive.
trades.catalyst: OpenAI-AWS GPU deal (MSFT-backed OpenAI); Iren-MSFT $9.7B GB300 GPU deal announced Nov 3; AI infrastructure capex acceleration narrative; November seasonality (+1.8% avg)
trades.lesson: MSFT was one of worst-hit Magnificent 7 stocks in November. Fell from 514 to 466 (-9%). AI capex narrative overshadowed by: (1) scrutiny of AI spending ROI, (2) hawkish Fed removing growth-at-any-price multiple, (3) tech selloff post-NVDA earnings sell-the-news. Buying big-cap tech at all-time highs into a hawkish Fed pivot is high risk — rate-cut backstop was being removed.
trades.what_market_was_actually_trading: November 2025 was when AI spending ROI scrutiny intensified. Every Magnificent 7 except Alphabet posted losing week in late November. Market asked when AI capex translates to earnings. MSFT particularly vulnerable as high-multiple AI story stock at all-time highs.

period_key_events.2025-11-03: OpenAI-AWS $38B GPU deal; Iren-MSFT $9.7B GB300 deal; Nasdaq +0.46%, S&P +0.17%, Dow -0.48%
period_key_events.2025-11-04: AMD Q3 earnings after close: $9.2B rev (+36% YoY), EPS $1.20 adj — beat. Q4 guide $9.6B above $9.17B consensus
period_key_events.2025-11-05: AMD stock recovered post-earnings (243 open → 256 close). Market digesting AI deal news
period_key_events.2025-11-06: AMD sell-the-news begins (253 open → 237 close, -7%). VST reports Q3: Adj EBITDA +$143M, Comanche Peak 20yr PPA, Perry NRC extension
period_key_events.2025-11-07: CEG reports Q3: EPS $3.04 adj (+11%), but guidance midpoint $9.25 below $9.42 consensus. VST bounces (178→190). US government shutdown in 37th day
period_key_events.2025-11-10: Market partially recovers. PLTR still elevated at ~193. Tech derating concerns building
period_key_events.2025-11-11: Veterans Day — stock market OPEN, bond market CLOSED. Settlement delays noted
period_key_events.2025-11-12: S&P +0.2% to 6,846. Healthcare, Staples, Energy led. Tech -0.9%. AMD bounces to 258 on MSFT chip order reports
period_key_events.2025-11-13: MAJOR SELLOFF: Hawkish regional Fed presidents (Boston, Cleveland, St. Louis). Dec cut odds collapse ~90%→~50%. S&P -1%+, third time in two weeks. Bitcoin -20% from Oct highs. Government shutdown ends (Trump signs Nov 13)
period_key_events.2025-11-14: Continued selloff. SPY hit 659 low. Tech derating deepening. NVDA earnings due Nov 19 looming as key catalyst
period_key_events.2025-11-19: NVDA Q3 FY2026 reported: $57B revenue (+62% YoY), Data Center $51.2B (+66%), EPS $1.30. Q4 guide $65B (+14% QoQ). SELL-THE-NEWS — stock sold off violently
period_key_events.post_period_context: NVDA fell from ~187 pre-earnings to 172-178 range by Nov 24-25. AMD continued falling to 206 by Nov 21. S&P recovered from Nov 14 lows. Healthcare dominated November performance (+9.3%)

regime_analysis.dominant_regime: AI multiple compression + hawkish Fed repricing
regime_analysis.what_worked: Shorting high-multiple AI/tech names (AMD, PLTR) as earnings-already-priced-in; contrarian long on oversold broad index post-shutdown resolution (SPY)
regime_analysis.what_failed: Long AI infrastructure plays (NVDA, MSFT); long nuclear/power names (CEG, VST, NRG) against hawkish rate headwind; mean reversion long in downtrend (AMD dip buy)
regime_analysis.key_insight: November 2025 was defined by a regime shift from AI-driven risk-on to hawkish Fed repricing. The moment to recognize the shift was Oct 29 FOMC hawkish press conference. All long AI/tech positions entered Nov 3 were fighting the regime. The two winning trades (AMD short, PLTR short) and the SPY long post-shutdown resolution all had clear regime-consistent logic.
regime_analysis.f15_signal: S&P 500 November winners/losers showed extreme sector divergence: Healthcare +9.3% vs Tech -4.3%. This was a sector rotation regime, not a broad bull market — concentration into defensives/healthcare, out of growth/tech/energy.
regime_analysis.common_mistake: Entered 4 longs on Nov 3 and 5 into a deteriorating macro regime (hawkish Fed, earnings priced in, AI ROI scrutiny). Should have recognized the regime change from Oct 29 FOMC and positioned short/neutral tech, long defensives.
